title: Blocked by CORS: who's stopping the request, and who can fix it?

mcq.question_md: Your React app on `localhost:3000` calls your Spring Boot API on `localhost:8080`. The console shows *"blocked by CORS policy"* — yet the same URL works perfectly in Postman. What's going on, and where does the fix belong?
options: The API server is down; restart Spring Boot and it will work
The browser blocks cross-origin responses unless the server allows the frontend's origin — fix it in the Spring Boot CORS config
React can't call APIs on a different port; move the frontend to port 8080
It's a React bug; wrapping the fetch in a try/catch will fix it
mcq.explanation_md: CORS is a **browser** security rule: JavaScript from one origin (`localhost:3000`) can't read responses from another origin (`localhost:8080`) unless the *server* explicitly allows it — for example with `@CrossOrigin(origins = "http://localhost:3000")` or a global CORS config in Spring. That's why the fix lives on the backend even though the error appears in the frontend console. The giveaway that kills option A: Postman works. Postman isn't a browser, so it doesn't enforce CORS — the server is up and answering fine. And a try/catch (option D) only changes how the failure is reported; the browser still refuses to hand your code the response.
